title: Autumn Durald Arkapaw Makes Oscar History as First Woman Cinematographer - PBS NewsHour
text: Autumn Durald Arkapaw, a 46-year-old American cinematographer, achieved a historic milestone at the 98th Academy Awards by becoming the first woman to win the Best Cinematography Oscar. She received the award for her work on director Ryan Coogler's film Sinners. Arkapaw's cinematography credits include notable projects such as Black Panther: Wakanda Forever, The Last Showgirl, and a Rihanna music video. She studied art history at Loyola Marymount University before pursuing cinematography at the American Film Institute, leveraging her photography background. While the cinematography profession has progressed significantly since Arkapaw began her career, women have remained underrepresented. Before her win, only three women had received Oscar nominations in this category: Rachel Morrison (2018), Ari Wegner (2019), and Mandy Walker (2021). Arkapaw was notably the first woman of color to be nominated. She emphasized the importance of visibility, stating: You need to see you to be you and expressed hope that inspiring younger women in the industry would follow. Sinners marked another cinematography milestone—no woman had previously shot a movie on IMAX film. Arkapaw combined IMAX film with Ultra Panavision 70 to tell the story effectively. Upon accepting her award, she expressed gratitude to the women who supported her career journey and highlighted her collaboration with director Coogler and her family.
